ABFS ({{ include "abfs.chart" . }}) installed into namespace {{ .Release.Namespace }}.

{{ if not (include "abfs.licensed" .) -}}
==> PHASE A (not yet licensed)
    Only the service accounts and pusher ConfigMap were rendered. The server,
    uploaders, and bootstrap Job stay gated off until licensed=true.

    1. Submit this runtime service account in the ABFS EAP form — it is the node SA
       of the dedicated ABFS node pool (see docs/02 §1b):
         {{ include "abfs.runtimeServiceAccount" . | default (printf "%s@%s.iam.gserviceaccount.com" (.Values.runtimeServiceAccountName | default "RUNTIME_SA") .Values.projectId) }}
    2. When the license arrives, base64 it into the `abfs-data` node pool's instance
       metadata (key abfs-license; see docs/02 §1b), then start ABFS (PHASE B):
         helm upgrade {{ .Release.Name }} <chart> -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} \
           -f values.yaml --set licensed=true
{{- else -}}
==> PHASE B (licensed) — ABFS is starting.

    Image:                         {{ include "abfs.image" . }}
    Server endpoint (in-cluster):  {{ include "abfs.server.name" . }}:{{ .Values.server.service.port }}
    {{ if .Values.server.service.internalLoadBalancer -}}
    Internal LB IP for clients:    kubectl get svc -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} {{ include "abfs.server.name" . }} -o wide
    {{- end }}

    Watch rollout:
      kubectl get pods -n {{ .Release.Namespace }}
      kubectl logs -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} job/abfs-pusher-config    # bootstrap result
      kubectl get statefulset -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} {{ include "abfs.uploader.name" . }}
    {{ if .Values.tests.enabled }}
    Smoke-test server connectivity:
      helm test {{ .Release.Name }} -n {{ .Release.Namespace }}
    {{- end }}
    {{ if .Values.vpa.enabled }}
    VPA right-sizing recommendations (updateMode={{ .Values.vpa.updateMode }}):
      kubectl describe vpa -n {{ .Release.Namespace }} {{ include "abfs.server.name" . }}
    {{- end }}

    Reminder: the ABFS server does NOT create the Spanner schema — the deploy applies it
    declaratively via infra/20-spanner.yaml (SpannerDatabase.spec.ddl), gated by the
    CREATE_TABLES toggle. See docs/03-spanner-schema-ownership.md.
    The ABFS data-plane pods run privileged + hostPID for the casfs mount — the
    namespace must permit it (Pod Security Admission = privileged).
{{- end }}
